heading: Definitions
label: 1683 Definitions

children.heading: Appropriate congressional committees
children.label: 1 Appropriate congressional committees
children.text: The term “appropriate congressional committees” means the Committee on Finance of the Senate and the Committee on Ways and Means of the House of Representatives.

children.heading: Country of export
children.label: 2 Country of export
children.text: The term “country of export” means the country (including any political subdivision of the country) from which softwood lumber or a softwood lumber product is exported before entering the United States.

children.heading: Customs laws of the United States
children.label: 3 Customs laws of the United States
children.text: The term “customs laws of the United States” means any law or regulation enforced or administered by U.S. Customs and Border Protection.

children.heading: Export charges
children.label: 4 Export charges
children.text: The term “export charges” means any tax, charge, or other fee collected by the country from which softwood lumber or a softwood lumber product, described in , is exported pursuant to an international agreement entered into by that country and the United States.section 1683b(a) of this title

children.label: i
children.text: In the case of softwood lumber or a softwood lumber product that has undergone only primary processing, the value that would be determined F.O.B. at the facility where the product underwent the last primary processing before export.

children.label: I
children.text: In the case of softwood lumber or a softwood lumber product described in subclause (II), the value that would be determined F.O.B. at the facility where the lumber or product underwent the last primary processing.

children.label: aa
children.text: does not hold tenure rights provided by the country of export;

children.label: bb
children.text: did not acquire standing timber directly from the country of export; and

children.label: cc
children.text: is not related to the person who holds tenure rights or acquired standing timber directly from the country of export.

children.label: I
children.text: In the case of softwood lumber or a softwood lumber product described in subclause (II), the value that would be determined F.O.B. at the facility where the product underwent the last processing before export.

children.label: aa
children.text: holds tenure rights provided by the country of export;

children.label: bb
children.text: acquired standing timber directly from the country of export; or

children.label: cc
children.text: is related to a person who holds tenure rights or acquired standing timber directly from the country of export.

children.label: i
children.text: the person bears a relationship to such other person described in ;section 152(a) of title 26

children.label: ii
children.text: the person bears a relationship to such other person described in section 267(b) of such title, except that “5 percent” shall be substituted for “50 percent” each place it appears;

children.label: iii
children.text: the person and such other person are part of a controlled group of corporations, as that term is defined in section 1563(a) of such title, except that “5 percent” shall be substituted for “80 percent” each place it appears;

children.label: iv
children.text: the person is an officer or director of such other person; or

children.label: v
children.text: the person is the employer of such other person.

children.heading: Tenure rights
children.label: C Tenure rights
children.text: For purposes of this paragraph, the term “tenure rights” means rights to harvest timber from public land granted by the country of export.

children.heading: Export price where F.O.B. value cannot be determined
children.label: D Export price where F.O.B. value cannot be determined
I
children.text: The market price for the lumber or a product sold at substantially the same level of trade as the exported lumber or product but in different quantities.

children.label: II
children.text: The market price for the lumber or a product sold at a different level of trade than the exported lumber or product but in similar quantities.

children.label: III
children.text: The market price for the lumber or a product sold at a different level of trade than the exported lumber or product and in different quantities.

children.heading: Level of trade
children.label: ii Level of trade
children.text: For purposes of clause (i), “level of trade” shall be determined in the same manner as provided under section 351.412(c) of title 19, Code of Federal Regulations (as in effect on ).January 1, 2008

children.heading: F.O.B.
children.label: 6 F.O.B.
children.text: The term “F.O.B.” means a value consisting of all charges payable by a purchaser, including those charges incurred in the placement of merchandise on board of a conveyance for shipment, but does not include the actual shipping charges or any applicable export charges.

children.heading: HTS
children.label: 7 HTS
children.text: The term “HTS” means the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States () (as in effect on ).19 U.S.C. 1202January 1, 2008

children.heading: Person
children.label: 8 Person
children.text: The term “person” includes any individual, partnership, corporation, association, organization, business trust, government entity, or other entity subject to the jurisdiction of the United States.

children.heading: United States
children.label: 9 United States
children.text: The term “United States” means the customs territory of the United States, as defined in General Note 2 of the HTS.
